steps.title: Identify the contrast
steps.content:
"While some commentators lauded... less ______ observers countered..." The first group holds an optimistic view (transparency is good).

The second group holds a critical, pessimistic view (blogs exacerbate polarization).

The word "less" indicates we need a positive quality that the critical group lacks.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean "reduce or address" — gaining access to more data from more countries lessened the geographic shortcoming. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — validate: confirm or prove correct — would mean researchers proved the flaw was justified, the opposite of fixing it. ✗
  • Choice B — presuppose: assume in advance — implies the shortcoming was assumed beforehand, not corrected later. ✗
  • Choice C — categorize: classify into groups — labeling the shortcoming doesn't address it. ✗
  • Choice D — mitigate: lessen the severity of — gaining new data reduced the myopic focus; fits exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
